headline: A Look At PayPal Holdings (PYPL) Valuation After CEO Exit Board Change And New Lawsuit
summary: PayPal Holdings (PYPL) is back in focus after CEO Alex Chriss departed, Alyssa Henry joined the board, and a securities class action lawsuit was filed over past growth guidance and execution. See our latest analysis for PayPal Holdings. Those leadership changes and the lawsuit are playing out against a weak share price backdrop, with a 90 day share price return of 23.55% and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 33.18% decline, signaling fading momentum despite new partnerships and crypto...

headline: Coinbase clears key regulatory hurdle in bid to bolster its stablecoin business
summary: Coinbase has received conditional approval from the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to operate as a trust bank, the company said Thursday.

raw.headline: Coinbase clears key regulatory hurdle in bid to bolster its stablecoin business

headline: PayPal (PYPL) Invests $400M to Revive Checkout Growth
summary: PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:PYPL) ranks among the stocks that could 10x over the next 5 years. On March 10, PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:PYPL) provided its 2026 forecast at the Wolfe FinTech Forum, highlighting CEO Enrique Lores’ emphasis on execution, product investment, and monetization. The company indicated that restoring the branded checkout remains a top priority, […]

headline: American Water Offers Convenient Ways for Customers to Pay Their Bills
summary: American Water (NYSE: AWK), the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the U.S., wants to remind customers that it provides secure and convenient bill payment options through its online portal, MyWater. The company recently broadened its platform to accept Apple Pay, Google Pay, Venmo, PayPal or PayPal Credit when enrolling in its Auto Pay program or making a one-time payment to make it easier for customers to pay their bills.

headline: Stablecoins roll on as rules evolve
summary: PayPal, Convera and Nium are among the companies racing to incorporate stablecoins in cross-border plays, even as regulators voice caution.

headline: FTC probes debanking practices at PayPal, Stripe, Visa and Mastercard
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has contacted leading payment providers, including PayPal, Stripe, Visa and Mastercard, to examine the growing issue of debanking.
